title: ws.rs: SEALED_WHISPER consumes rate-limit quota before subscription check — unsubscribed clients can drain their rate budget
description:
In relay/src/ws.rs the SEALED_WHISPER handler checks rate-limit BEFORE checking subscription (lines ~1477-1518):

  1. check_rate_limit() — consumes a token
  2. pub_id format validation
  3. subscription check

Compare with WHISPER (lines ~1335-1459):

  1. pub_id format validation
  2. subscription check  (before rate limit)
  3. check_rate_limit()

For SEALED_WHISPER, an unsubscribed client can repeatedly call SEALED_WHISPER (with any valid-format recipient pub_id) and burn through their rate-limit budget even though the subscription gate will always block the actual message. Once the rate-limit budget is consumed, the same client cannot perform other rate-limited operations (GET_KEY_PACKAGE, PUBLISH_KEY_PACKAGE, BROADCAST, etc.) that they ARE legitimately allowed to do without a subscription.

This is inconsistent with WHISPER (subscription checked first, no rate-limit consumed), BROADCAST (subscription checked first), GROUP_SEND (subscription checked first), and GROUP_CREATE (subscription checked first).

Fix: move the subscription check before check_rate_limit in SEALED_WHISPER, matching the ordering in WHISPER.
